title: The Millennium

jokes.text: I'd like to buy these huaraches.
jokes.setup: Jerry being completely ignored by saleswoman who's having personal conversation
jokes.punchline: His polite attempt to actually conduct business in a store
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational moment about retail rudeness, economically delivered

jokes.text: Nothing. You just lost a customer.
jokes.setup: Saleswoman finally asks what she can do for him after ignoring him
jokes.punchline: Jerry's understated but cutting response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry delivery - polite but devastating, great economy of words

jokes.text: In fact, I enjoy being the theater cutup. Last week after a preview, I yelled out, 'Must miss!'
jokes.setup: George claiming he's a theater cutup who loves previews
jokes.punchline: His pathetically unfunny opposite of 'must see'
jokes.explanation: Perfect George delusion - thinks he's clever but reveals how unfunny he is

jokes.text: I think that I was in that theater. That was really funny.
jokes.setup: George's proud story about his terrible joke
jokes.punchline: Elaine's sarcastic encouragement of his delusion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Elaine's sarcastic nature

jokes.text: You've got to lift up that plastic thing with a pen.
jokes.setup: Discussion about being on speed dial after two dates
jokes.punchline: Jerry's specific observation about the mundane difficulty of changing speed dial
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld minutiae observation - finding comedy in the tiniest details

jokes.text: New Year's Eve, 1999, the millennium.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if Kramer will keep chairs for eight months
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's actually for 2.5 years in the future
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer logic - planning absurdly far ahead for a party

jokes.text: I got a case of party poppers I'm gonna keep in front of them.
jokes.setup: Jerry's disbelief about keeping chairs for years
jokes.punchline: Kramer's additional ridiculous storage plan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Kramer's absurd planning

jokes.text: I'm on a winning ball club, Jerry. They probably want to pick my brain.
jokes.setup: George being invited to lunch by Mets executives
jokes.punchline: His delusional belief they value his baseball expertise
jokes.explanation: Classic George delusion about his own importance and competence

jokes.text: I don't mind the Mayans.
jokes.setup: Elaine complaining about 'those Mayans' at the store
jokes.punchline: Jerry missing that she means the people, not the ancient civilization
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection joke about Jerry's literal interpretation

jokes.text: And he's 1/64 Mayan.
jokes.setup: Kramer recommending Cinco de Mayo and mentioning Marcelino
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and tiny fraction of Mayan heritage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist detail - the precision of 1/64 makes it funnier

jokes.text: Those aren't for New Year's. Those are my everyday balloons.
jokes.setup: Jerry pointing out balloons won't stay inflated until New Year's
jokes.punchline: Kramer's casual revelation about having everyday balloons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer absurdity - having 'everyday balloons' is both unexpected and perfectly in character

jokes.text: We're not talking. We're just talking.
jokes.setup: George asking if they're talking about getting him fired
jokes.punchline: The executives' contradictory non-denial denial
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay about corporate doublespeak and plausible deniability

jokes.text: We couldn't say that we said it.
jokes.setup: Continuing the non-denial about getting George fired
jokes.punchline: Even more convoluted corporate speak
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the doublespeak joke

jokes.text: Maybe a nicer girl called.
jokes.setup: Jerry checking messages while on a date
jokes.punchline: Saying this cruel thought out loud to his date
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry cruelty - saying the internal thought out loud

jokes.text: Sales commission, bye-bye-o.
jokes.setup: Kramer showing off his Cinco de Mayo purchases
jokes.punchline: His playful rhyme about defeating Putumayo
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Kramer's playful nature

jokes.text: I want to walk away from the Yankees with people saying: 'Wow! Now, that guy got canned.'
jokes.setup: George needing to get fired to get the Mets job
jokes.punchline: His aspiration to be memorably incompetent
jokes.explanation: Great George logic - wanting to be spectacularly bad at failing

jokes.text: So you want to go out in a final blaze of incompetence.
jokes.setup: George's desire to be memorably fired
jokes.punchline: Jerry's perfect summation with 'blaze of incompetence'
jokes.explanation: Perfect phrase construction - 'blaze of incompetence' is brilliant wordplay on 'blaze of glory'

jokes.text: Or is this mouth-vacuum thing for real?
jokes.setup: Kramer wondering about napkins in the year 2000
jokes.punchline: His bizarre futuristic eating method concept
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer futurism - completely absurd but delivered with conviction

jokes.text: It's pronounced 'thermometer.'
jokes.setup: Jerry asking what a barometer is exactly
jokes.punchline: George's confident but completely wrong correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - confidently wrong about something basic

jokes.text: You just think of a person, they'll be talking to you.
jokes.setup: Kramer predicting future technology
jokes.punchline: His description of telepathic communication
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer futurism, though less distinctive than his other predictions

jokes.text: You forgot the gift certificate to Barnes & Noble.
jokes.setup: Valerie mentioning all the nice things Jerry did
jokes.punchline: Adding another expensive gesture to the list
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation showing Jerry's expensive dating tactics

jokes.text: Seinfeld, you magnificent bastard.
jokes.setup: Jerry seeing he's number one on speed dial
jokes.punchline: Patton movie reference applied to his dating success
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference showing Jerry's self-satisfaction

jokes.text: Isn't that Babe's Ruth uniform?
jokes.setup: George showing up with stolen Yankees memorabilia
jokes.punchline: His casual reference to wearing Babe Ruth's uniform
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of George's sabotage attempt

jokes.text: It's good. Juicy this time of year.
jokes.setup: George eating strawberries while wearing Babe Ruth's uniform
jokes.punchline: His casual food critique while desecrating baseball history
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment - treating uniform desecration as casual snack time

jokes.text: Will people be able to breathe under water in the year 2000?
jokes.setup: Kramer planning his millennium party
jokes.punchline: His concern about aquatic breathing at his party
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer absurdity - planning for impossible future abilities

jokes.text: Oh, no wonder we're getting so much rain.
jokes.setup: Elaine's rain dance-like protesting outside Putumayo
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic explanation for the weather
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor connecting Elaine's dancing to weather

jokes.text: Come celebrate the millennium with 'Newmannium.'
jokes.setup: Newman sending party invitations
jokes.punchline: His portmanteau of Newman and millennium
jokes.explanation: Good Newman ego moment - making the millennium about himself

jokes.text: It's taken me 13 years to climb up to the top of that speed dial.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Hamilton confronting Jerry about the speed dial
jokes.punchline: Her obsessive dedication to speed dial positioning
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the speed dial subplot's absurdity

jokes.text: He wasn't really a sultan, huh? What do you make of that?
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner ranting about Babe Ruth
jokes.punchline: His confusion about 'Sultan of Swat' being literal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Steinbrenner moment - taking a nickname literally

jokes.text: You don't think that nerve disease of his was contagious, do you?
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner wearing Lou Gehrig's pants
jokes.punchline: His concern about catching ALS from clothing
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about ALS, typical Steinbrenner ignorance

jokes.text: My stepmother got to you, didn't she?
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to get off the speed dial
jokes.punchline: Valerie immediately knowing her stepmother interfered
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing this is a recurring family dynamic

jokes.text: Think again, long shanks.
jokes.setup: Kramer asking Newman to cancel his party
jokes.punchline: Newman's dismissive insult for Kramer
jokes.explanation: Good Newman insult - 'long shanks' is both accurate and dismissive

jokes.text: I started planning this in 1978. I put a deposit down on that restaurant that overlooks Times Square. And I booked Christopher Cross.
jokes.setup: Newman defending his party planning
jokes.punchline: The absurd 21-year planning timeline and dated entertainment choice
jokes.explanation: Excellent Newman moment - obsessive planning with terrible musical taste

jokes.text: For me, the next millennium must be Jerry-free.
jokes.setup: Newman's condition for letting Kramer cohost
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration about the next thousand years
jokes.explanation: Perfect Newman grandiosity - planning Jerry hatred for 1000 years

jokes.text: Never thought I'd fail at failing.
jokes.setup: George unable to get himself fired
jokes.punchline: The paradox of being too incompetent to be incompetent enough
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paradox - perfect George philosophy about incompetence

jokes.text: I'm Gladys Mayo.
jokes.setup: Elaine thinking she's confronting rival store owner
jokes.punchline: Revealing she owns both competing stores
jokes.explanation: Good plot twist revealing the absurdity of Elaine's protest

jokes.text: This really sticks in my craw.
jokes.setup: Discovering she's been manipulated by store owner
jokes.punchline: Her old-fashioned expression of frustration
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment with archaic expression

jokes.text: Why don't I put you on my speed dial?
jokes.setup: Mrs. Hamilton seducing Jerry while Valerie is absent
jokes.punchline: Using speed dial as sexual proposition
jokes.explanation: Good callback using established subplot for seduction scene

jokes.text: Newmannium!
jokes.setup: Newman celebrating at his millennium party
jokes.punchline: His triumphant shout of his party name
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established Newman ego moment

jokes.text: A short, stocky, bald man is streaking across the field.
jokes.setup: George's attempt to get fired by streaking
jokes.punchline: The clinical sports announcer description
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag with announcer's matter-of-fact tone

jokes.text: He's wearing a flesh-toned body stocking. Apparently he's a bit bashful.
jokes.setup: George streaking to get fired
jokes.punchline: Revealing his modest approach to streaking
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - even his rebellion is timid and self-conscious

jokes.text: To the 'Kramennium.'
jokes.setup: Kramer and Newman making peace about the party
jokes.punchline: Kramer's version of the portmanteau party name
jokes.explanation: Decent callback creating parity between the characters

jokes.text: That's interesting because as everyone knows, since there was no year zero, the millennium doesn't begin until the year 2001, which would make your party one year late and thus quite lame.
jokes.setup: Newman bragging about booking for the millennium
jokes.punchline: Jerry's pedantic correction demolishing Newman's achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry moment - using pedantic knowledge to devastate Newman's pride

jokes.text: I don't feel well at all. I feel all dried-out inside.
jokes.setup: Kramer's desiccant removal from clothes
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Hamilton literally drying out from wearing the clothes
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the desiccant subplot with literal consequences

jokes.text: I hit the button for Poison Control and I get you.
jokes.setup: Emergency with Mrs. Hamilton, Valerie hits speed dial
jokes.punchline: Jerry being programmed as Poison Control
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Jerry ends up in the most important speed dial position by accident

jokes.text: Wow, Poison Control. That's even higher than number one.
jokes.setup: Jerry discovering he's programmed as Poison Control
jokes.punchline: His satisfaction at achieving the ultimate speed dial status
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Jerry's shallow priorities satisfied in the most absurd way

tmdb_overview: The Mets express interest in George; an inattentive saleswoman irks Elaine; Kramer prepares for his millennium party; Jerry jockeys for position on his girlfriend's speed dial.
